# ai-host - SEO + GEO Remediation Playbook

**Target**: https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai
**Backing service**: aicart/intelliverse-ai-ws (image `intelliverse-ai:latest`)
**Source repo (best guess)**: intelli-verse-x/intelliverse-ai
**Audit reference**: `reports/ai-host.md`
**Generated**: 2026-05-17T18:15:00Z
**Generated by**: gastown audits/crew/auditor (firecrawl + claude)

---

## 1. Executive summary

The ai-host service is **completely invisible to search engines and AI systems**. It returns HTTP 426 "Upgrade Required" for all standard HTTP requests, blocking all crawlers, indexing bots, and LLM knowledge systems. This is the worst possible SEO/GEO configuration—the site effectively does not exist on the public web. The headline fix is to add a standard HTTP landing page at the root path with full SEO metadata while preserving the WebSocket app on a sub-route. Projected impact: 0/10 → 7/10 LLM-friendliness; transition from "completely undiscoverable" to "indexed and answerable" by search engines and AI assistants within 2-4 weeks of deployment.

## 2. Current state recap

From `reports/ai-host.md`, all checks FAIL due to HTTP 426:

- ✗ **HTTP accessibility** ([report#L10](reports/ai-host.md#L10)): Returns HTTP 426 "Upgrade Required" — site requires WebSocket/protocol upgrade and is inaccessible to standard HTTP clients (CRITICAL)
- ✗ **title tag** ([report#L11](reports/ai-host.md#L11)): N/A — cannot audit, no HTML returned
- ✗ **meta description** ([report#L12](reports/ai-host.md#L12)): N/A — cannot audit, no HTML returned
- ✗ **canonical** ([report#L13](reports/ai-host.md#L13)): N/A — cannot audit, no HTML returned
- ✗ **robots.txt** ([report#L14](reports/ai-host.md#L14)): N/A — cannot audit, connection requires upgrade
- ✗ **sitemap.xml** ([report#L15](reports/ai-host.md#L15)): N/A — cannot audit, connection requires upgrade
- ✗ **HSTS** ([report#L16](reports/ai-host.md#L16)): N/A — no standard HTTP response headers
- ✗ **Open Graph** ([report#L17](reports/ai-host.md#L17)): N/A — cannot audit, no HTML returned
- ✗ **Twitter Card** ([report#L18](reports/ai-host.md#L18)): N/A — cannot audit, no HTML returned
- ✗ **JSON-LD schema** ([report#L19](reports/ai-host.md#L19)): N/A — cannot audit, no HTML returned
- ✗ **h1 count** ([report#L20](reports/ai-host.md#L20)): N/A — cannot audit, no HTML returned
- ✗ **lang attribute** ([report#L21](reports/ai-host.md#L21)): N/A — cannot audit, no HTML returned
- ✗ **CSP** ([report#L22](reports/ai-host.md#L22)): N/A — no standard HTTP response headers

**GEO findings** ([report#L24-L40](reports/ai-host.md#L24-L40)): AI-answerability FAIL (0/10). An AI assistant asked "what is ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai for?" cannot answer from any publicly available information.

## 3. 2026 best-practice baseline (researched via firecrawl)

### SEO fundamentals

**Source**: [Google Search Central - SEO Starter Guide](https://developers.google.com/search/docs/fundamentals/seo-starter-guide) (2026)

> "Help Google find your content... Check if Google can see your page the same way a user does. Use the URL Inspection tool in Search Console to see how Googlebot sees your page."

**Key requirement**: Pages must return HTTP 200 with crawlable HTML. HTTP 426 blocks all indexing.

**Title and meta description**: "Influence your title links... Google Search generates a title link for each result based primarily on the page's main heading... Create descriptive page titles that clearly indicate what the page is about."

**Canonical URLs**: "Reduce duplicate content... Use canonical URLs to indicate which version of a page you want to appear in search results."

### Crawler control

**Source**: [Google Search Central - robots.txt](https://developers.google.com/search/docs/crawling-indexing/robots/intro)

> "A robots.txt file tells search engine crawlers which URLs the crawler can access on your site."

**Best practice 2026**: Serve `/robots.txt` with HTTP 200, include `Sitemap:` directive pointing to `sitemap.xml`.

### Structured data

**Source**: [Schema.org - Full Hierarchy](https://schema.org/docs/full.html) (2026)

**SoftwareApplication** schema is appropriate for web app services:
- `@type: "SoftwareApplication"`
- `applicationCategory`, `offers`, `name`, `description`

**Open Graph** (og:title, og:description, og:type, og:url) and **Twitter Card** (twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description) are standard for social sharing.

### Security signals

**Source**: [web.dev - Security headers](https://web.dev/articles/security-headers) (2026)

> "HTTPS is table stakes... but HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) tells browsers to *only* connect via HTTPS even if the user types http://."

**Required headers** (2026 baseline):
- `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains`
- `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`
- `Referrer-Policy: no-referrer-when-downgrade`
- `Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'` (or stricter)

### Server-side rendering

**Source**: [Google Search Central - JavaScript SEO](https://developers.google.com/search/docs/crawling-indexing/javascript/javascript-seo-basics)

> "Googlebot can render and understand JavaScript-based websites, but there are some caveats... server-side rendering (SSR) or pre-rendering helps ensure Googlebot sees your content."

**2026 status**: Most LLM crawlers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Perplexity) execute JavaScript but prefer server-rendered content. If content requires WebSocket, it's invisible.

### GEO/LLM-readability

**Source**: [llms.txt specification](https://llmstxt.org) (2026)

> "Large language models increasingly rely on website information, but face a critical limitation: context windows are too small to handle most websites in their entirety... We propose adding a `/llms.txt` markdown file to websites to provide LLM-friendly content."

**Best practice**: Serve `/llms.txt` with:
- H1: project name
- Blockquote: short summary
- Sections with markdown links to `.md` versions of key docs

**Semantic HTML**: Use proper heading hierarchy (h1 > h2 > h3), `<main>`, `<article>`, `<section>` for LLM content extraction.

**FAQ Schema**: For FAQ sections, use `FAQPage` JSON-LD to surface question-answer pairs.

## 4. Concrete remediation (prioritized)

### 1. Separate HTTP landing page from WebSocket app

- **Priority**: P0 (CRITICAL) — estimated 4-8 hours
- **What**: Modify server routing to serve HTTP 200 landing page at `/`, reserve WebSocket upgrade for `/app` or `/ws`
- **Why**: Linked to all FAIL rows in audit — HTTP 426 blocks all crawling/indexing ([report#L10](reports/ai-host.md#L10))
- **Where**: Likely `intelliverse-ai/src/server.js` or `intelliverse-ai/nginx.conf` (need to inspect repo)
- **How**: 

**Option A: Application-level routing** (if Node.js/Express WebSocket server):

```javascript
// intelliverse-ai/src/server.js
const express = require('express');
const { WebSocketServer } = require('ws');
const path = require('path');

const app = express();
const server = require('http').createServer(app);
const wss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true });

// Serve static landing page at root
app.get('/', (req, res) => {
    res.sendFile(path.join(__dirname, '../public/index.html'));
});

// Serve SEO files
app.get('/robots.txt', (req, res) => {
    res.type('text/plain').sendFile(path.join(__dirname, '../public/robots.txt'));
});

app.get('/sitemap.xml', (req, res) => {
    res.type('application/xml').sendFile(path.join(__dirname, '../public/sitemap.xml'));
});

app.get('/llms.txt', (req, res) => {
    res.type('text/plain').sendFile(path.join(__dirname, '../public/llms.txt'));
});

// WebSocket upgrade ONLY on /app route
server.on('upgrade', (request, socket, head) => {
    if (request.url === '/app') {
        wss.handleUpgrade(request, socket, head, (ws) => {
            wss.emit('connection', ws, request);
        });
    } else {
        socket.destroy();
    }
});

server.listen(8080);
```

**Option B: Nginx reverse proxy** (if nginx fronts the WebSocket service):

```nginx
# /etc/nginx/sites-available/ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai.conf
server {
    listen 443 ssl http2;
    server_name ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai;
    
    ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai/fullchain.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai/privkey.pem;
    
    # Security headers
    add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains" always;
    add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;
    add_header Referrer-Policy "no-referrer-when-downgrade" always;
    
    # Serve static landing page at root
    location = / {
        root /var/www/ai-host-public;
        try_files /index.html =404;
    }
    
    # SEO files
    location ~ ^/(robots\.txt|sitemap\.xml|llms\.txt)$ {
        root /var/www/ai-host-public;
    }
    
    # WebSocket app on /app
    location /app {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:8080;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade";
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
    }
}
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -I https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep "HTTP/2 200"
curl -sS https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep -i "<title>AI Host"
```

### 2. Create SEO-optimized landing page

- **Priority**: P0 (CRITICAL) — estimated 2-4 hours
- **What**: Build HTML landing page with full SEO metadata (title, meta description, OG tags, JSON-LD, h1)
- **Why**: Fixes missing metadata ([report#L11-L22](reports/ai-host.md#L11-L22)), enables indexing
- **Where**: `intelliverse-ai/public/index.html` (new file)
- **How**:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>AI Host | Real-Time AI Assistant Platform - IntelliVerse X</title>
    <meta name="description" content="AI Host provides real-time AI assistance through an advanced conversational interface. Connect instantly with intelligent AI agents for chat, support, and automation.">
    
    <link rel="canonical" href="https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai/">
    
    <!-- Open Graph -->
    <meta property="og:title" content="AI Host | Real-Time AI Assistant Platform">
    <meta property="og:description" content="Real-time AI assistance through an advanced conversational interface. Connect with intelligent agents for chat, support, and automation.">
    <meta property="og:type" content="website">
    <meta property="og:url" content="https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai/">
    <meta property="og:site_name" content="IntelliVerse X">
    
    <!-- Twitter Card -->
    <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">
    <meta name="twitter:title" content="AI Host | Real-Time AI Assistant">
    <meta name="twitter:description" content="Real-time AI assistance platform with advanced conversational AI.">
    
    <!-- JSON-LD Structured Data -->
    <script type="application/ld+json">
    {
      "@context": "https://schema.org",
      "@type": "SoftwareApplication",
      "name": "AI Host",
      "applicationCategory": "CommunicationApplication",
      "description": "Real-time AI assistant platform providing intelligent conversational interfaces for chat, support, and automation.",
      "url": "https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai/",
      "offers": {
        "@type": "Offer",
        "price": "0",
        "priceCurrency": "USD"
      },
      "featureList": [
        "Real-time AI chat",
        "WebSocket-based communication",
        "Multi-agent support",
        "Streaming responses"
      ]
    }
    </script>
    
    <style>
        body {
            font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif;
            max-width: 800px;
            margin: 0 auto;
            padding: 2rem;
            line-height: 1.6;
        }
        h1 { color: #1a1a1a; }
        .cta {
            display: inline-block;
            background: #0066cc;
            color: white;
            padding: 0.75rem 1.5rem;
            text-decoration: none;
            border-radius: 4px;
            margin-top: 1rem;
        }
        .features { list-style: none; padding: 0; }
        .features li:before { content: "✓ "; color: #0066cc; font-weight: bold; }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <main>
        <h1>AI Host - Real-Time AI Assistant Platform</h1>
        
        <p>AI Host provides powerful real-time AI assistance through an advanced conversational interface. Connect instantly with intelligent AI agents for chat, customer support, and workflow automation.</p>
        
        <h2>Key Features</h2>
        <ul class="features">
            <li>Real-time conversational AI with streaming responses</li>
            <li>WebSocket-based instant communication</li>
            <li>Multi-agent orchestration and support</li>
            <li>Low-latency, high-performance architecture</li>
            <li>Scalable infrastructure for enterprise workloads</li>
        </ul>
        
        <h2>Use Cases</h2>
        <ul class="features">
            <li>Interactive AI chatbots and virtual assistants</li>
            <li>Real-time customer support automation</li>
            <li>Developer tools and code assistance</li>
            <li>Educational tutoring and Q&A systems</li>
        </ul>
        
        <a href="/app" class="cta">Launch AI Host Application</a>
        
        <h2>About IntelliVerse X</h2>
        <p>AI Host is part of the IntelliVerse X ecosystem, providing cutting-edge AI-powered tools and platforms. Visit <a href="https://intelli-verse-x.ai">intelli-verse-x.ai</a> to explore our full suite of AI solutions.</p>
    </main>
</body>
</html>
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -sS https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep -E '<title>|<meta name="description"|application/ld\+json|<h1>'
firecrawl scrape https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai --only-main-content -o /tmp/ai-host-post.md
wc -l /tmp/ai-host-post.md  # expect > 30 lines of content
```

### 3. Add robots.txt

- **Priority**: P0 (CRITICAL) — estimated 15 minutes
- **What**: Create `/robots.txt` to allow crawler indexing and reference sitemap
- **Why**: Fixes missing crawler control ([report#L14](reports/ai-host.md#L14))
- **Where**: `intelliverse-ai/public/robots.txt` (new file)
- **How**:

```
User-agent: *
Allow: /
Disallow: /app

# AI crawlers
User-agent: GPTBot
Allow: /

User-agent: ChatGPT-User
Allow: /

User-agent: Claude-Web
Allow: /

Sitemap: https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai/sitemap.xml
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -sS https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai/robots.txt | grep "Sitemap:"
```

- **What**: Create XML sitemap listing the landing page

- **Where**: `intelliverse-ai/public/sitemap.xml` (new file)

```xml

<urlset xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9">
  <url>
    <loc>https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai/</loc>
    <lastmod>2026-05-17</lastmod>
    <changefreq>monthly</changefreq>
    <priority>1.0</priority>
  </url>
</urlset>
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -sS https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai/sitemap.xml | head -10
```

- **Why**: Improves GEO/LLM-answerability ([report#L24-L40](reports/ai-host.md#L24-L40))

```markdown
# AI Host

> AI Host is a real-time AI assistant platform providing WebSocket-based conversational interfaces for chat, customer support, and workflow automation.

Key characteristics:

- **Architecture**: WebSocket-based for low-latency streaming responses
- **Use cases**: Interactive chatbots, customer support automation, developer tools, educational tutoring
- **Technology**: Multi-agent orchestration with scalable infrastructure
- **Access**: Web application interface at https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai/app

## Documentation

- [Main landing page](https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai/): Overview of features and use cases

## Related Services

- [IntelliVerse X Platform](https://platform.intelli-verse-x.ai): Main user platform
- [IntelliVerse X Home](https://intelli-verse-x.ai): Ecosystem overview
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -sS https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai/llms.txt | head -20
```

### 6. Add security headers to responses

- **Priority**: P1 — estimated 30 minutes
- **What**: Configure HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, CSP headers
- **Why**: Fixes missing security signals ([report#L16,L22](reports/ai-host.md#L16)), improves SEO ranking
- **Where**: Nginx config (see remediation #1 Option B) OR application middleware
- **How** (if application-level):

```javascript
// intelliverse-ai/src/server.js
app.use((req, res, next) => {
    res.setHeader('Strict-Transport-Security', 'max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains');
    res.setHeader('X-Content-Type-Options', 'nosniff');
    res.setHeader('X-Frame-Options', 'DENY');
    res.setHeader('Referrer-Policy', 'no-referrer-when-downgrade');
    res.setHeader('Content-Security-Policy', "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline';");
    next();
});
```

- **Verify**:
```bash
curl -sSI https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep -iE 'strict-transport|x-content-type|x-frame|referrer-policy|content-security'
```

</div>

</div>

<script type="application/ld+json">
{

      "name": "What is AI Host?",

      "@type": "Question",

      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",

      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "AI Host is designed for interactive AI chatbots, real-time customer support automation, developer tools and code assistance, and educational tutoring systems requiring low-latency responses."
      }

- **Verify**:

## 5. Stack-aware notes

Based on the service name (`intelliverse-ai-ws`) and WebSocket behavior, this appears to be a **Node.js WebSocket server** (likely using `ws` or `socket.io`).

**Key implementation patterns**:

1. **Do NOT break the existing WebSocket app** — preserve all existing `/app` functionality. The remediation adds a parallel HTTP landing page, it does not replace the WebSocket service.

2. **Static file serving**: If the codebase doesn't already have an HTTP server, add Express middleware:
   ```javascript
   const express = require('express');
   app.use(express.static('public'));
   ```

3. **URL structure** (recommended):
   - `GET /` → HTTP 200 landing page (new)
   - `GET /robots.txt` → HTTP 200 robots file (new)
   - `GET /sitemap.xml` → HTTP 200 sitemap (new)
   - `GET /llms.txt` → HTTP 200 llms file (new)
   - `GET /app` (with `Upgrade: websocket` header) → HTTP 101 Switching Protocols (existing)

4. **Deployment**: If this service is deployed via Kubernetes (k8s namespace `aicart/intelliverse-ai-ws`), ensure:
   - The ConfigMap or deployment spec mounts the new `public/` directory
   - The Service exposes port 8080 (or whatever port the HTTP server listens on)
   - The Ingress routes traffic to the new HTTP endpoints (not just WebSocket upgrade)

5. **Alternative: Nginx sidecar** — If modifying the application code is complex, deploy an Nginx sidecar container in the same pod that:
   - Serves static files from a shared volume
   - Proxies `/app` to `localhost:8080` (the WebSocket service)
   - Handles all HTTP 200 responses for `/`, `/robots.txt`, etc.

## 6. Verification plan

After deploy, run:

```bash
# 1. HTTP accessibility (must return 200, not 426)
curl -I https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai
# Expected: HTTP/2 200

# 2. Security headers
curl -sSI https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep -iE 'strict-transport|x-content-type|x-frame|referrer-policy|content-security'
# Expected: 5 header matches

# 3. Structured data
curl -sS https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep -ciE 'application/ld\+json|og:|twitter:|<link rel="canonical"'
# Expected: > 10 matches

# 4. Crawler files
curl -sS https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai/robots.txt | head -5
# Expected: "User-agent: *"

curl -sS https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai/sitemap.xml | head -5
# Expected: "<?xml version="

curl -sS https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai/llms.txt | head -5
# Expected: "# AI Host"

# 5. SEO metadata
curl -sS https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai | grep -E '<title>|<meta name="description"'
# Expected: 2 matches

# 6. GEO answerability
firecrawl scrape https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai --only-main-content -o /tmp/ai-host-post.md
wc -l /tmp/ai-host-post.md
# Expected: > 50 lines (before fix: 0 lines)

diff /tmp/ai-host-pre.md /tmp/ai-host-post.md | wc -l
# Expected: > 100 lines of new content

# 7. WebSocket app still works
wscat -c wss://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai/app
# Expected: WebSocket connection established (existing functionality preserved)
```

**Target Lighthouse scores** (run after SEO fixes deployed):

```bash
lighthouse https://ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai --only-categories=seo,best-practices,accessibility --output=json
```

- **SEO**: 90+ (currently: unmeasurable due to HTTP 426)
- **Best Practices**: 90+ (add security headers, HTTPS only)
- **Accessibility**: 85+ (semantic HTML, proper heading hierarchy)

**Search Console verification** (4-6 weeks post-deploy):
- Submit sitemap to Google Search Console
- Monitor "Coverage" report — expect 1 indexed page (landing page)
- Check "Performance" report — expect impressions/clicks from organic search

## 7. Open questions / blockers

1. **Who owns the intelliverse-ai repo?** — Need access to verify actual stack (Node.js, Python, Go?) and file structure. If repo is private or access is restricted, coordinate with the team that maintains this service.

2. **Is there existing marketing content?** — The landing page content in remediation #2 is generic. If marketing/product team has approved copy, feature descriptions, or screenshots, substitute those for better conversion.

3. **Do we want to add `/llms.txt`?** — This is a newer standard (2026). If the organization is conservative about adopting new specs, this can be deprioritized to P2 or skipped. However, it's low effort (1 hour) and high GEO impact.

4. **Current deployment method?** — Need to understand CI/CD pipeline:
   - Is this deployed via Kubernetes manifests in a separate repo?
   - Are static files baked into the Docker image or mounted from ConfigMap?
   - What's the rollback process if the routing change breaks the WebSocket app?

5. **Is the HTTP 426 behavior intentional?** — Confirm with the original developers whether returning HTTP 426 at the root was a deliberate design choice (unlikely) or an oversight. If it's intentional, understand the reasoning before proposing changes.

6. **Do we need authentication on the landing page?** — The remediation assumes the landing page is public (for SEO). If the entire service should be behind auth, the SEO strategy needs to pivot to an authenticated landing page with enough public metadata for crawlers (see Google's guidance on authenticated content).

## 8. Estimated impact

**SEO**:
- **Before**: 0/10 — site is invisible to Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, and all other search engines due to HTTP 426
- **After**: 7/10 — indexed landing page with proper metadata, sitemap, and robots.txt
- **Improvement**: Site goes from "completely undiscoverable" to "indexed with proper metadata"
- **Indexing timeline**: Google typically crawls and indexes new pages within 3-7 days after submitting sitemap; full ranking impact visible in 2-4 weeks

**GEO** (LLM-friendliness):
- **Before**: 0/10 — AI systems (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, etc.) have ZERO information about this service
- **After**: 7/10 — LLMs can answer "What is ai-host.intelli-verse-x.ai?" with accurate feature descriptions, use cases, and access method
- **Improvement**: LLM knowledge systems can extract content from the landing page, FAQ schema, and `/llms.txt` for answering user queries
- **Indexing timeline**: LLM-specific crawlers (GPTBot, Claude-Web) typically re-index monthly; expect GEO improvements visible in 4-8 weeks

**Time-to-value**:
- **Search engines**: 2-4 weeks until organic search traffic appears (assumes sitemap submitted to Search Console)
- **AI assistants**: 4-8 weeks until LLMs have updated knowledge (depends on crawler frequency and training data cutoffs)
- **Direct impact**: Immediate improvement in referral traffic from social media (Open Graph tags enable rich link previews)

**Projected traffic lift**:
- **Before**: 0 organic search visitors/month (site is uncrawlable)
- **After**: 50-200 organic visitors/month within 3 months (assumes modest search volume for "AI Host" + "real-time AI assistant" + brand queries)
- **Long-term**: As content expands (add blog, docs, tutorials), organic traffic can grow to 500+ visitors/month

**Note**: These projections assume the remediation is implemented fully (all P0 + P1 items). Partial implementation will yield proportionally lower impact.
